questions.q: In which year was Satyajit Ray's debut film Pather Panchali released?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray's debut feature Pather Panchali was released in 1955.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray was primarily associated with which language industry of Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray was the most celebrated director of Bengali cinema.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray's first three films Pather Panchali, Aparajito and Apur Sansar are collectively known as what?
questions.explanation: These three films form Satyajit Ray's celebrated Apu Trilogy.

questions.q: In which year did Satyajit Ray receive an honorary Academy Award (Oscar) for lifetime achievement?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray received an honorary Academy Award in 1992, shortly before his death.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray was a filmmaker, writer, illustrator and what else?
questions.explanation: Besides directing, Satyajit Ray composed the music for many of his own films from the early 1960s onward.

questions.q: Pather Panchali, Satyajit Ray's debut, was adapted from a novel by which Bengali author?
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali was based on Bibhutibhushan Bandopadhyay's novel of the same name.

questions.q: The young boy at the centre of Satyajit Ray's Apu Trilogy is named what?
questions.explanation: The trilogy follows the life of the boy Apu from childhood to adulthood.

questions.q: In which Indian city was Satyajit Ray born and based for most of his career?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray was born in and worked out of Calcutta (now Kolkata).

questions.q: Roughly how many films did Satyajit Ray direct over his four-decade career?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray directed roughly 36 films across his career.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray created which fictional Bengali detective who appears in several of his stories and films?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray created the detective Feluda (Pradosh Chandra Mitra).

questions.q: Who composed the sitar-based musical score for Satyajit Ray's Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: Ravi Shankar composed the music for Pather Panchali.

questions.q: For which film did Satyajit Ray win the Silver Bear for Best Director at the Berlin Film Festival in 1965?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray won the Silver Bear for Best Director at Berlin for Charulata in 1965.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray's 1964 film Charulata was based on a novella by which author?
questions.explanation: Charulata was adapted from Rabindranath Tagore's novella 'Nastanirh' (The Broken Nest).

questions.q: Which actor played the adult Apu in Satyajit Ray's Apur Sansar and went on to star in many of his films?
questions.explanation: Soumitra Chatterjee played the adult Apu and became Ray's most frequent leading man.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray's only full-length Hindi feature film, set in Lucknow on the eve of the 1857 annexation of Awadh, is which of the following?
questions.explanation: Shatranj Ke Khilari (The Chess Players, 1977) was Satyajit Ray's only full-length Hindi feature.

questions.q: Which 1958 Satyajit Ray film centres on a decaying aristocratic landlord obsessed with hosting music recitals?
questions.explanation: Jalsaghar (The Music Room) depicts a declining zamindar devoted to musical soirees.

questions.q: Shatranj Ke Khilari by Satyajit Ray was adapted from a short story by which Hindi-Urdu author?
questions.explanation: Shatranj Ke Khilari was based on Premchand's short story of the same name.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray's children's fantasy musical featuring two performers granted magical boons by the King of Ghosts is titled what?
questions.explanation: Goopy Gyne Bagha Byne (1969) features the musicians Goopy and Bagha and their magical boons from the King of Ghosts.

questions.q: In Satyajit Ray's Nayak (1966), Bengali matinee idol Uttam Kumar plays which kind of character?
options: A celebrated film star travelling by train
questions.explanation: In Nayak (1966) Uttam Kumar plays a famous movie star who reflects on his life during a train journey to Delhi.

questions.q: Before becoming a filmmaker, Satyajit Ray worked in which profession in Calcutta?
options: Commercial artist at an advertising agency
questions.explanation: Ray worked as a commercial artist and graphic designer at the British-run advertising agency D.J. Keymer.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray's debut Pather Panchali won the 'Best Human Document' award at which film festival in 1956?
questions.explanation: Pather Panchali won the Best Human Document award at the 1956 Cannes Film Festival.

questions.q: Which Satyajit Ray film won the Golden Lion (top prize) at the Venice Film Festival in 1957?
questions.explanation: Aparajito, the second film of the Apu Trilogy, won the Golden Lion at Venice in 1957.

questions.q: Which cinematographer shot Satyajit Ray's Apu Trilogy and is credited with pioneering the bounce-lighting technique?
questions.explanation: Subrata Mitra shot the Apu Trilogy and several other early Ray films, and is credited with pioneering bounce lighting in cinema.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray was deeply influenced by which Italian neorealist film, which he saw in London before making Pather Panchali?
questions.explanation: Seeing Vittorio De Sica's Bicycle Thieves in London in 1950 convinced Ray to make realistic films on location.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray's grandfather Upendrakishore Ray Chowdhury and father Sukumar Ray were renowned in Bengal for what?
questions.explanation: Both were celebrated writers and illustrators of Bengali children's literature, with Sukumar Ray famed for his nonsense verse.

questions.q: Which film society did Satyajit Ray co-found in Calcutta in 1947, exposing him to world cinema before he became a director?
questions.explanation: Ray co-founded the Calcutta Film Society in 1947, which exposed him to a wide range of international cinema.

questions.q: Satyajit Ray's early-1970s 'Calcutta Trilogy' comprises Pratidwandi, Seemabaddha and which third film?
questions.explanation: Jana Aranya (1976) completes Ray's loosely linked Calcutta Trilogy alongside Pratidwandi (1970) and Seemabaddha (1971).

questions.q: Satyajit Ray's final film, released in 1991, was titled what?
questions.explanation: Agantuk (The Stranger), released in 1991, was Satyajit Ray's last completed film.

questions.q: Which Satyajit Ray feature about the 1943 Bengal famine, based on a Bibhutibhushan Bandopadhyay novel, won the Golden Bear at Berlin in 1973?
questions.explanation: Ashani Sanket (Distant Thunder), about the 1943 Bengal famine, won the Golden Bear at Berlin in 1973.

questions.q: India's highest civilian award, the Bharat Ratna, was conferred on Satyajit Ray in which year?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 1992, the year of his death.
